P = 30

s = 36

q = 18

r = 24

12 years?

Given, (7x+10) = (6x+10) + 10 => x = 10

(10) = 70

Deepak's age after 12 years = 70+12 = 82

And, age of Ali = 6x = 6
(10) = 60

Ali’s age after 12 years = 60+12 = 72

Hence, required ratio = 82:72 = 41:36

The average age of a group of 60 students is 15

years. If 3 students whose ages are 17 years, 24

years and _____ years is replaced by 3 new

students whose ages are ____ years, 22 years

and 31 years, then the new average age of the

group increases by 0.5 years.

The values given in which of the following

options will fill the blanks in the same order in

which is it given to make the above statement

true:

true:
explanation:
Since, the new average increases by 0.5 years.

So, sum of the ages of the added students –

sum of the ages of the students who left = 60 ×

0.5 = 30 years

Option A:

(45 + 22 + 31) – (17 + 24 + 27) = 30 years

So, option A can be the answer.

Option B:

(64 + 22 + 31) – (17 + 24 + 44) = 32 years

So, option B cannot be the answer.

Option C:

(37 + 22 + 31) – (17 + 24 + 19) = 30 years

So, option C can be the answer.

Hence, option c.
